latest.title: The Agentic Shift: Why Enterprises Will Need Fewer Applications  —  But also Different Ones
latest.description: How Agentic AI has potential to rationalize the bloated enterprise application portfolio.

latest.title: Week 3 Assignment: EC2 (Ubuntu) + IAM + Security + Billing | AWS Cloud Club Unilorin Bootcamp 2026
latest.description:
Completed Week 3 Hands-On Assignment for AWS Cloud Club Unilorin Bootcamp 2026!
Built a fully secure Ubuntu EC2 web server with Nginx, created IAM users + group with custom least-privilege policy (allow start/stop/reboot, deny terminate), set up $5 monthly billing alerts, attached IAM role, and validated permissions with Access Denied proof.
#AWS #CloudClubUnilorin #Bootcamp2026 #EC2 #IAM

latest.title: AIdeas Finalist: AI Runtime Assurance
latest.description: AI Runtime Assurance is a black-box recorder for AI agents on AWS. It captures HTTP requests, AWS API actions, and file operations, then turns them into one session-level security report with evidence, severity scores, and plain-English explanations. It uses Amazon Bedrock when available and falls back cleanly when it is not.

latest.title: Implement monthly data refresh for an Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL database with reduced downtime
latest.description: Explained how to automate the completely fresh data loading of an Aurora database at the end of each month, which is essential for maintaining an up-to-date and accurate database

latest.title: Performing internal Vulnerability Assessment on a private EC2 instance using Tenable Nessus in AWS
latest.description:
This guide demonstrates how to deploy and scan a target EC2 instance inside a private subnet using Tenable Nessus. It covers instance configuration, application deployment using user data, secure access via AWS Systems Manager, hostname resolution, and credentialed scanning setup.

By the end of this lab, you will have a working internal scanning setup that reflects common cloud security practices in AWS environments.

latest.title: AWS Interconnect- last mile, streamlining private connectivity to AWS.
latest.description: BUT, didn’t we have that service capability already?

latest.title: AIdeas Finalist：Remember Me — An Intelligent Memory Support System for Alzheimer’s Disease
latest.description: Remember Me is an AI-powered assistive system dedicated to restoring dignity, independence, and emotional connection for people living with Alzheimer’s disease and dementia. Through gentle, intuitive, human-centered design, it turns confusion into clarity, loneliness into comfort, and dependence into confidence — delivering real, sustainable social good for vulnerable communities worldwide.

latest.title: My Journey Through EC2, IAM and Cost Governance
latest.description: Using the billing and cost management feature for creating budgets with multiple alerts; launching an Ubuntu server; giving proper permissions to IAM groups, and users, through IAM policies; attaching a custom IAM role to the Ubuntu server and finally deploying a webpage on the Ubuntu server.

latest.title: Agentic AI with Claude on AWS Hands-on Builder Workshop - Automotive/Manufacturing Industry Edition
latest.description:
Unlock the Power of Claude Code in Amazon Bedrock — an immersive workshop for builders, architects, and technical leaders. Limited to 100 qualified builders.

This hands-on workshop is crafted with the deliberate goal to build your understanding of Claude Code progressively, showing you not just what it can do, but how and why it works. It is designed to give you both the strategic context and technical depth to get started with Claude today.

latest.title: AIdeas Finalist: NeuroPath AI
latest.description: An Adaptive Learning Ecosystem for the Neurodivergent Mind

latest.title: AIdeas Finalist: LikenessGuard - World’s First Multi-Agent, Edge-Capable Consent Enforcement Platform with Cross-Platform Integration and Cryptographic Proof-of-Face
latest.description: LikenessGuard enforces consent before any AI generates or edits an image involving a real person’s likeness. Built with multi-agent Bedrock, edge enforcement, Proof-of-Face, and live integrations with Claude and Grok, it prevents harm at the source and will be open-sourced after April 30, 2026, so platforms and regulators can adopt it as a responsible AI framework.

latest.title: Migrate your containerized application from AWS App Runner to Amazon ECS Express Mode
latest.description: A step-by-step guide to migrating containerized workloads from AWS App Runner to Amazon ECS Express Mode, with IAM roles, CLI commands, and DNS cutover.

latest.title: Building Production-Ready Agents with Kiro: Steering, Powers, Hooks, Specs, and Skills
latest.description: Learn how Kiro's five core features work together to give AI agents persistent context, on-demand tooling, and automated guardrails with a real-time fraud detection walkthrough for financial services.

latest.title: Breaking the Glass Ceiling of Low-Code: Migrating Automations to AWS
latest.description: Outgrowing low-code/no-code platforms? Learn how to migrate your workflows to AWS. We explore mapping webhooks and actions to API Gateway, Lambda, and Step Functions as an example.

latest.title: AWS PROJECT
latest.description: This shows the description on all the tasks I underwent

latest.title: How I built and configure EC2 instance with ubuntu vm, created IAM user, and created a billing alarm
latest.description: This is an article about my experience while working on my AWS cloud club unilorin bootcamp week 3 tasks where I talked about configuration of billing alert, IAM user creation, and running server on ubuntu server in my ec2 insatnce.

latest.title: OpenClaw on AWS Lightsail: Live Demo, Real Findings and the Security Gap No Framework Models Yet — Part 4
latest.description: Live demo closing a 4-part security series on OpenClaw deployed on AWS Lightsail. Real setup obstacles, 13 validated findings across IaaS and application layers, and prompt injection + memory poisoning tested against Claude Sonnet 4.6. Includes the security gap MITRE ATLAS and OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026 don't cover yet: how IaaS configuration decisions activate or disable application-layer threat vectors.

latest.title: Building an Event-Driven, AI-Powered Driver Monitoring System on AWS with Amazon Bedrock
latest.description: Driver fatigue and distraction are among the leading causes of accidents in the logistics and transportation sectors. While traditional fleet management systems track vehicle location and speed, they often fail to monitor the most critical component: the driver’s real-time state.

latest.title: A SECURE BUILD: EC2 (Ubuntu) + IAM + Security + Billing
latest.description: From Zero to Deployed: Securing AWS with IAM, EC2, and Nginx on Ubuntu [AWS Cloud Club Unilorin Bootcamp 2026]

trending.title: AIdeas Finalist: VERA, A Multi-Agent Triage System for Healthcare in Chile
trending.description: A multi-agent healthcare triage system that screens patients in Chilean Spanish, detects crisis signals, and generates clinical reports. Built by two people on AWS for $17/month, designed to integrate with Chile's public health system via HL7 FHIR.

trending.title: AIdeas Finalist: Ivy - The World's First Offline-Capable, Proactive AI Tutoring Agent
trending.description: A proactive AI tutoring agent powered by Amazon Bedrock that monitors student progress, initiates outbound voice calls, and runs on-device inference for offline learning addressing educational inequity for millions of Ethiopian students with intermittent connectivity.

trending.title: Introducing Article View Counts on AWS Builder Center
trending.description: You can now see how many builders are reading your articles, giving you a real metric to understand the reach of your content.
